phase1.explanation:
Custom exceptions let you create domain-specific error types:

Why create custom exceptions?
• Clearer error messages
• Domain-specific error types
• Better error handling with specific catches

Types of custom exceptions:

1. Checked (extends Exception):
   • Must be handled or declared
   • For recoverable errors
   • Example: InsufficientFundsException

2. Unchecked (extends RuntimeException):
   • No handling required
   • For programming errors
   • Example: NegativeAmountException

Creating a custom exception:
• Extend Exception or RuntimeException
• Call super(message) in constructor
• Can add custom fields/methods

Best practices:
• Provide meaningful error messages
• Use checked for recoverable errors
• Use unchecked for programming errors
• Include cause for chaining

Naming convention: [ErrorType]Exception (e.g., InsufficientFundsException)

phase2.errorMessage:
MyException.java:9: error: cannot find symbol
    void validate(int value) throws MyException {  // ERROR!
                                    ^
  symbol:   class MyException
  location: class Validator
MyException.java:11: error: incompatible types: MyException cannot be converted to Throwable
            throw new MyException("Invalid value");
            ^
2 errors
phase2.explanation:
MyException doesn't extend Throwable (Exception or RuntimeException), so it can't be thrown!

Custom exceptions MUST extend Exception or RuntimeException (or their subclasses).

FIX: class MyException extends Exception

Or: class MyException extends RuntimeException

Always extend the proper parent class!

phase3.prompt:
Create a custom exception class AgeException (extends Exception).

Create a Person class with:
• Fields: name, age
• Constructor validates:
  - If age < 0 or age > 150 → throw AgeException
• Method: vote() — can only vote if age >= 18 → throw AgeException if not

In main, test with valid and invalid ages.
dialogueHints: Person constructor: check age range, throw if invalid
vote(): check age >= 18, throw if not
phase3.openingDialogue: Custom exceptions — your own error language! 🎨
